title: Brachiaria xantholeuca
intro: Brachiaria xantholeuca, also known as Xantholeucus grass, is a tropical grass species native to the Americas. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Brachiaria xantholeuca include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Brachiaria xantholeuca is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in the form of essential oils or capsules for its various health benefits.
taxonomy: Brachiaria xantholeuca is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Brachiaria, which is commonly known as button grass or signal grass. The genus Brachiaria is further classified under the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. Brachiaria xantholeuca is native to Australia and is known for its bright yellow inflorescences. It is a highly variable species with multiple subspecies found across its native range.
